summary: Validated GitHub push readiness after git filter-repo + video compression. 5 of 7 checks PASS. Filesystem clean (no files >100MB), git history clean (largest blob is 5084e72e...mp4 at 94.94 MB, safely under GitHub's 100 MB hard limit), all 3 compressed videos are valid H.264 and served correctly via backend (200 OK, video/mp4, Content-Length matches). HOWEVER 2 of the claimed fixes were NOT actually applied prior to this iteration: (1) /app/.gitignore was missing all three new entries (/bin/, /app/bin/, .git.backup-*), and (2) bin/ffmpeg + bin/ffprobe + bin/test.txt are STILL tracked in the git index. I fixed (1) by appending the three entries to /app/.gitignore — verified via `git check-ignore --no-index`. Issue (2) requires main agent action because untracking already-committed files requires a new commit (or a fresh history rewrite) which is out of scope for testing.

git_history_blob_over_100mb.detail: Largest blob in entire history is backend/uploads/gallery/5084e72e854f40a48ca0c015dffd499a.mp4 at 99,549,003 bytes (94.94 MB) — under GitHub's 104,857,600 byte limit. Top 5: 5084e72e...mp4 99549003, 24177a7f...mp4 68806789, d252a0f8...mp4 65734716, bin/ffmpeg 51134160, bin/ffprobe 50994160

bin_folder_not_tracked.detail: git ls-files bin/ still returns bin/ffmpeg, bin/ffprobe, bin/test.txt. Adding to .gitignore alone does NOT untrack — main agent must `git rm --cached bin/ffmpeg bin/ffprobe bin/test.txt` and commit. NOTE: these are <100MB so do not block the push, but they violate the stated requirement and waste ~102 MB in the next push.

current_videos_tracked.detail: All 3 compressed MP4s (91e7dfe..., 5084e72e..., d252a0f8...) are tracked in HEAD

gitignore_entries.detail: BEFORE: /app/.gitignore was missing /bin/, /app/bin/, .git.backup-*. AFTER: appended all three entries; verified by `git check-ignore --no-index` matching all three patterns. NOTE: claim in problem statement that these were added was inaccurate prior to this iteration.

video_integrity.detail: ffprobe shows codec_name=h264 for all three: 91e7dfe...(1920x1080), 5084e72e...(1920x1440), d252a0f8...(1280x960)

http_reachability.detail: All three videos served via https://repo-to-deploy.preview.emergentagent.com/api/uploads/gallery/<name> with HTTP/2 200, content-type: video/mp4, and content-length matching on-disk size (14765743, 21376189, 65734716 respectively)

action_items: MAIN AGENT: Untrack the /bin/ folder by running: `cd /app && git rm --cached bin/ffmpeg bin/ffprobe bin/test.txt && git commit -m 'untrack local ffmpeg binaries'` BEFORE the next GitHub push. Without this, push will succeed (files <100MB) but will include 102 MB of ffmpeg/ffprobe binaries in the new commit.
MAIN AGENT: After untracking bin/, the local copies at /app/bin/ffmpeg and /app/bin/ffprobe will still exist on disk (because of --cached) so video compression tooling keeps working.

USER FACING: As stated in original problem statement, since history was rewritten via git-filter-repo, the user MUST push to a NEW branch (e.g. `clean-history`) or recreate the GitHub remote repo — the Emergent 'Save to GitHub' UI uses non-force push which will be rejected by a diverged remote.
critical_code_review_comments: The fix narrative in the problem statement claimed three .gitignore entries were added, but a `tail` of /app/.gitignore before this iteration confirmed they were absent. Verify .gitignore actually gets committed when fixes are applied.
Untracking bin/ via .gitignore alone is a common mistake — gitignore is only consulted for UNtracked files. Tracked files remain tracked until `git rm --cached`.
Consider adding bin/ files to the same filter-repo invalidation pass next time, otherwise they continue to ship in every push (51 MB ffmpeg + 51 MB ffprobe = ~102 MB extra per branch push).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_7 confirmed git history is push-safe (no blobs >=100MB) and all three compressed videos play & serve correctly. Two follow-ups remain for main agent: (a) `git rm --cached bin/ffmpeg bin/ffprobe bin/test.txt` then commit; (b) user must push to a fresh branch or recreated remote due to history rewrite. After (a), re-run `git ls-files bin/` — must be empty.
rca of the issue: Original 'Failed to push to GitHub' was caused by 134MB + 254MB blobs in git history exceeding GitHub's 100MB per-file hard limit. Mitigation via `git filter-repo --strip-blobs-bigger-than 100M` succeeded — verified by enumerating every blob in `git rev-list --objects --all` and finding max size = 99,549,003 bytes (94.94 MB). However the secondary mitigation (adding /bin/, /app/bin/, .git.backup-* to .gitignore AND untracking bin/) was only partially applied: .gitignore entries were missing (now fixed by tester); bin/ files remain in the index (must be fixed by main agent before next push).
